edges.explanation: 9% real cut per pupil with 14-28% cuts to post-16 is the direct evidence that funding is insufficient
The scale of cuts across every phase proves education was cut more than almost any other service
Post-16 funding cut 14-28% and never recovered is the specific evidence that the skills pipeline is running on fumes
Fees losing 30% real value proves the current system is broken even if fee abolition is regressive
The 30% real-value loss is what justifies restoring fees with inflation indexing rather than scrapping them
Widening disadvantage gap since the pandemic proves funding insufficiency is hitting the most vulnerable hardest
The widening gap is the direct justification for tripling the Pupil Premium targeted at evidence-based interventions
Apprenticeship starts falling after the levy proves the narrow levy wasn't working and broadening is needed
Constant skills reorganisation without resolving the fundamental problem proves chronic underinvestment in technical education
Falling apprenticeship starts justify the Growth and Skills Levy reform with quality standards
7-10% annual returns from early intervention is the Heckman evidence that early years has the highest ROI
Heckman's ROI evidence directly justifies the Sure Start 2.0 investment in integrated children's services
Below-OECD spending with no manifesto committing to adequacy proves education is underfunded and no one is fixing it
Below-comparator spending directly supports the claim that workforce crises trace to education underinvestment
Record vacancies, missed targets, and retention as the bigger problem proves recruitment alone can't solve the crisis
The workforce crisis directly justifies independent pay review and fully funded recommended rises
8-13% lifetime earnings return per year of education proves education investment has measurable economic returns
Returns to education justify above-inflation funding as an investment not just a cost
SEND deficits in the billions with underlying cost drivers unaddressed is a major component of the funding insufficiency
The SEND crisis directly justifies the national funding body reform within the school funding policy
Sure Start's measurable improvements prove that integrated early years services work when properly funded
Sure Start's demonstrated success is the evidence base for rebuilding it as Sure Start 2.0
Funding being insufficient directly supports the root node claim that education constrains all downstream domains
Skills levy needing reform supports the claim that workforce crises trace to skills system underinvestment
Early years having highest ROI but being underfunded supports the overall underinvestment claim
Teacher crisis driven by pay and conditions proves the system is failing because of underinvestment in its workforce
Teaching workforce crisis is itself a workforce problem caused by education system underinvestment — recursive failure
Above-inflation school funding depends on the claim that education is the underfunded root node constraining everything else
Skills reform depends on the claim that workforce crises trace to chronic underinvestment in technical education
The Retrofit Training Academy depends on the claim that workforce shortages block delivery in other domains
Restoring college funding depends on the claim that the skills pipeline is running on fumes after 14-28% cuts
Sure Start 2.0 depends on the finding that early years has the highest educational ROI
Independent pay review depends on the finding that pay and conditions are the binding constraint on the teacher crisis
Ofsted reform rather than abolition supports the broader school improvement framework within the funding policy
Protecting spending in real terms only maintains the inadequate status quo rather than the above-inflation increases the evidence requires
Closing university courses to fund apprenticeships is logically flawed and doesn't address the college funding collapse
6,500 new teachers is the right direction but without independent pay review the retention problem persists
3,000 nurseries in schools is the most specific early years delivery mechanism matching Sure Start 2.0
Growth and Skills Levy broadening aligns with the evidence-based skills reform direction
Above-inflation school and college funding matches the evidence-based minimum almost exactly
Independent Teachers' Review Body matches the evidence-based pay reform policy
Maintenance grants align with the expand-grants-rather-than-scrap-fees approach
Scrapping fees and cancelling debt contradicts the evidence that income-contingent loans are progressive — fee abolition would be regressive
Universal free school meals aligns with the early years investment priority though goes beyond the evidence-based position
Restricting undergraduate numbers and cutting university funding contradicts the evidence that education investment has measurable economic returns
Above-inflation school funding creates the fiscal context within which teacher pay reform can be delivered
School funding and early years expansion both draw from the education spending envelope, requiring prioritisation
Broader skills levy reform creates the framework within which specialist retrofit training can be delivered
Apprenticeship starts falling after the levy proves the Conservative plan to close courses and fund apprenticeships misunderstands the skills system problem
The logical flaw in closing courses to fund apprenticeships directly undermines the Conservative education policy
The finding that fee abolition is regressive but the system is broken supports the reform approach of restoring real value plus expanding grants
Teacher retention crisis driven partly by Ofsted accountability pressure supports the case for Ofsted reform
Tripling the Pupil Premium depends on the finding that investment in younger children produces the highest returns per pound
Fee reform with inflation indexing depends on the finding that abolition is regressive but the current freeze is unsustainable

cross_domain_edges.explanation: Chronic education underinvestment is why the NHS depends on overseas recruitment — domestic training pipeline was defunded
The Retrofit Training Academy creates the workforce the National Insulation Service requires — without training there are no installers
Retrofit training enables the housing domain's national insulation programme by producing the skilled workers it needs
The skills pipeline running on fumes is the root cause of the immigration dependency that the immigration domain identifies
Skills levy reform is the mechanism that makes the immigration domain's co-funded domestic training pact deliverable
Widening disadvantage gap feeding into lower lifetime earnings contributes to the welfare system being overwhelmed by low-wage in-work poverty
Education underfunding is a component of the dependency chain from failing services to fiscal dishonesty
